title: Verilator not handling individual generate/if blocks.
body: --- Author Name: **Udi Finkelstein** Original Redmine Issue: 1148 from https://www.veripool.org --- the following code produces a warning: ```module test; test2 #( .P(3)) t2 (); endmodule module test2 #(P=10); generate if (P>0) begin wire [P:0]x; end if (P > 0) begin assign x = {(P+1){1'b0}}; end endgenerate endmodule ``` ``` $ verilator --cc -sv test.sv %Warning-IMPLICIT: test.sv:14: Signal definition not found, creating implicitly: x %Warning-IMPLICIT: Use "/* verilator lint_off IMPLICIT */" and lint_on around source to disable this message. %Warning-WIDTH: test.sv:14: Operator ASSIGNW expects 1 bits on the Assign RHS, but Assign RHS's REPLICATE generates 4 bits. %Error: Exiting due to 2 warning(s) %Error: Command Failed /auto/csiproj1/udif/local/bin/verilator_bin --cc -sv test.sv ``` A slightly modified code: ```module test; test2 #( .P(3)) t2 (); endmodule module test2 #(P=10); generate if (P>0) begin wire [P:0]x; assign x = {(P+1){1'b0}}; end endgenerate endmodule ``` produces no warnings. Yes, this module does nothing, but it's a minimum complete verifiable example (mvce).
